expectations.text: Catches raw Tailwind palette colors used semantically (text-green-500 / text-yellow-500 / text-red-500 for status)
expectations.evidence: Row 7: 'Raw Tailwind palette classes (text-green-500, text-yellow-500, text-red-500) bypass the semantic color tokens'.

expectations.text: Catches the hardcoded hex color #7c3aed in the inline style attribute
expectations.evidence: Row 1: 'Inline hardcoded hex color #7c3aed (purple) via style attribute bypasses the design system'.

expectations.text: Catches the deprecated <Tag> component and recommends <Badge>
expectations.evidence: Row 8 flags Tag color misuse but does NOT identify <Tag> as deprecated, and recommends 'use a neutral color' rather than replacing with <Badge>.
Row 2: 'Uses low-level ButtonPrimitive ... Replace with <Button variant=solid color=brand>'.

expectations.text: Catches the raw <button> element with a custom SVG and recommends shared Button + Icon
expectations.evidence: Row 3: 'Raw <button> with an inline SVG for the delete action. Use <Button variant=ghost> <Icon iconName=trash>'.

expectations.text: Catches the missing aria-label on the icon-only delete button
expectations.evidence: Row 4: 'Icon-only button has no accessible name. Add aria-label=Delete service.'

expectations.text: Catches off-grid mid-range spacing (gap-5 and/or p-5)
expectations.evidence: Row 9: 'gap-5 and p-5 (20px) are off the typical 4/8/12/16/24 spacing rhythm. Align with p-4 gap-4.'
Row 5: 'focus:outline-none removes the keyboard focus indicator without providing a replacement. Fails WCAG 2.4.7.'

expectations.text: Catches the raw neutral scale class (border-neutral-300)
expectations.evidence: Row 11 mentions border-neutral-300 in passing as 'may also not match the standard border-neutral-200 divider token' but treats it as a color-match issue, not a raw-scale violation. Also explicitly recommends keeping a raw-scale token (border-neutral-200), so it would re-introduce the same anti-pattern.

expectations.text: Output uses a markdown table with severity labels ([blocking] / [important] / [nit])
expectations.evidence: Uses High/Medium/Low severity labels rather than the skill-defined [blocking]/[important]/[nit] format.

expectations.text: Output ends with a totals-by-severity summary
expectations.evidence: Has a prose Summary paragraph but no totals-by-severity count (e.g. no 'X blocking, Y important, Z nits').
